description_long: This is a vibrantly colored parrot with a predominantly red-orange head, neck, and upper body, transitioning to yellow and green on the wings, and deep blue on the flight feathers and tail. The bird has a large, pale, hooked beak typical of macaws, and a white facial patch with fine feather lines. Its legs and feet are dark gray and robust, adapted for perching. The tail is long and pointed, with blue and red hues blending toward the tip. The plumage is smooth and glossy, with distinct color transitions and a lifelike feather texture.
description: A vividly colored parrot with red, yellow, green, and blue plumage, a large hooked beak, and a long pointed tail.
description_view_0: The left side of the parrot is visible, showing the red head, yellow-green wing coverts, and blue tail feathers.
description_view_1: The right side is shown, highlighting the hooked beak, red-orange body, and the transition from yellow to blue on the wings and tail.
description_view_2: A rear-left view emphasizes the long blue tail, green-yellow wing coverts, and the layered feather arrangement.
description_view_3: A rear-right view displays the extended blue tail feathers, red back, and the gradient of colors on the wings.
attribute_plumage_pattern: Solid red on head and body, yellow and green on wing coverts, blue on flight feathers and tail, with smooth color transitions.
